frameworks.name: Bayesian model selection
frameworks.one_line: model evidence used to objectively choose between competing high-level parameter-dynamics models

frameworks.name: superstatistical random walks
frameworks.one_line: two-level process where short-timescale model has slowly fluctuating parameters

frameworks.name: hierarchical time-series modeling
frameworks.one_line: low-level data model with a high-level model governing parameter variation over time

core_question: How can one efficiently infer time-varying parameters of a low-level process and objectively select the high-level model that governs their fluctuations?
key_assumptions: time series have short-range correlations on short timescales
low-level data-generating model is known or dictated by data type
model parameters fluctuate on longer timescales according to an unknown high-level model
manipulations: candidate high-level models for parameter dynamics
application domain (finance, cell biology, occupational safety, climate)
key_findings: method recovers the time course of parameter variations from short-correlation series
model evidence discriminates between competing high-level dynamics models
detects anomalous price movements in financial markets
characterizes cancer cell invasiveness, coal-mine safety policy effects, and climate-change scenarios
interpretations: objective model comparison enables principled choice among high-level dynamics hypotheses
a single computationally efficient framework generalizes across diverse complex systems
